back_translation: Masters, treat your slaves justly and fairly, because you know that you yourselves also have a Master in heaven
Devote yourselves to prayer; be watchful in prayer together with thanksgiving
At the same time, pray also for us, so that God will open a door of the word for us, to proclaim the mystery of Lord Christ — which is the reason I am imprisoned
So that I may proclaim that gospel clearly, as I should speak it
Walk in life with wisdom toward outsiders; use the opportunity that you have to its full value
Let your speech be full of grace always, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how to answer each person
Tychicus will inform you of all matters concerning me; he is a beloved brother, a faithful servant, and a fellow-slave in Lord Jesus
I have sent him to you for this very purpose — so that you may know the news of us, and so that he may encourage your hearts
Together with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of yours; the two of them will inform you of every matter that has happened here
Aristarchus, my fellow-prisoner, sends greetings to you; and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas, also sends greetings; you have already received instructions about him — if he comes to you, welcome him

And Jesus, who is called Justus, also sends greetings; these three are Jewish believers who are circumcised, fellow-workers of mine for the kingdom of God only this many, and they have become a comfort to me

Epaphras, one of yours and a slave of Lord Jesus Christ, sends greetings to you; he wrestles in prayer for you always, so that you may stand firm as those who are mature and have full assurance in all the will of God
For I bear witness for him that he works hard for you, and for the people in the city of Laodicea and in the city of Hierapolis
Luke the beloved physician, and Demas, send greetings to you
Greet the brothers in the city of Laodicea, and Nympha, and the church that meets at her house
And when this letter has been read among you, see to it that there is also a reading in the church of the Laodiceans, and the letter from the city of Laodicea, you should also read
And tell Archippus: ‘Pay attention to the ministry that you have received in Lord Jesus, so that you may complete it’
This greeting is written by my own hand — Paul; remember my chains; may grace abide with you
